title: QUIC Server-Role Inbound Client Finished Proof Architecture
purpose: Describe the permanent server-role inbound client Finished proof slice that lives behind the existing transcript-progress and bridge-driver seam, consumes the complete local server flight through local Finished, derives the expected client Finished verify data from the managed transcript boundary, verifies that Finished cryptographically, publishes PeerFinishedVerified, and exposes PeerHandshakeTranscriptCompleted as the next honest server-role post-proof milestone without claiming server-role commit or interop support.
design_summary: The connection library keeps inbound client Finished proof as the next bridge-owned server-role slice after the existing local Finished continuation rather than adding a second TLS stack or a completion subsystem. The existing transcript-progress owner remains responsible for ordered Handshake CRYPTO assembly, deterministic message framing checks, and peer transport-parameter staging from ClientHello. Once that owner has accepted a supported ClientHello for TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1, the managed key-schedule owner has already published the local ServerHello, Handshake key availability, the local EncryptedExtensions continuation, the local Certificate continuation, the local CertificateVerify continuation, and the local Finished continuation. The inbound proof slice verifies the peer Finished against the transcript hash that includes the complete local server flight, publishes PeerFinishedVerified only after that verification succeeds, and lets the bridge publish PeerHandshakeTranscriptCompleted as the first honest server-side post-proof milestone. The slice deliberately stops short of peer transport-parameter commit, client-certificate authentication, trust-store policy, hostname/identity policy, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.
risks: The inbound client Finished proof must stay behind the existing bridge seam and must not grow into a broad TLS completion subsystem or a platform certificate-store integration.
The supported subset must stay pinned to TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1 until a later requirement explicitly widens it.
PeerFinishedVerified must not be mistaken for client identity, certificate trust, or any authorization claim beyond Finished possession.
Server-role commit must remain blocked even though PeerHandshakeTranscriptCompleted becomes an honest post-proof milestone after inbound client Finished verification succeeds.
